card.title: mazer: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Coordinate mobile safe-area, overlay containment, browser layout, and touch-control behavior through one viewport contract and physical-device proof path.
card.objective: Keep every Mazer surface contained and usable across desktop, narrow browser, phone browser, and installed PWA while preserving one deterministic control-intent contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
Mobile and browser layout work can be verified independently and together through a shared matrix.
No completion claim relies on desktop-only proof for a phone or restored-window defect.
Player Guide content remains contained and held stick/arrow intent remains recoverable on the physical-phone surface.
discoveries: The exact production UI packet is clean at 390x844 DPR 2 after excluding an unrelated known topology assertion for seed 3749.
Automated browser geometry and deterministic pointer proof cannot replace the operator's physical-iPhone control-feel acceptance.
The card must remain in Review at 84 percent until the deployed build is checked on the physical iPhone.
nextActions: Verify Player Guide left-edge containment on the production build using the operator's physical iPhone.
Verify one-tile wall fallback, dominant-lane T-junction steering, blocked-hold recovery, and sliding-arrow capture on the physical iPhone.
Keep installed-PWA banding and native browser-chrome/maximize proof open.
blockers: Physical iPhone post-release feel and containment verification is pending.
evidence: Vercel deployment dpl_3PGem7fCME4mAkUdM4h98MQefet3 is READY and aliased to https://fawxzzy-mazer.vercel.app.
The canonical production alias returned HTTP 200 with bundle assets/main-D7gXztN9.js.
The production 390x844 DPR-2 capture packet passed Menu, Options, Play, Pause, top/bottom scroll states, diagnostics, and zero page/console-error checks.
Vercel returned no runtime error logs for the release deployment.

entry.headline: Merged control repair is live in Mazer production
completed: Merged Mazer PR #77 into main through merge commit 8ea4eed42024914397573af83a63d8ee79c5ced1.
Deployed that exact clean merge to the existing Mazer production project.
Verified the production alias and release bundle over HTTP.
Passed the complete authenticated phone-sized UI surface packet against production.
discovered: The first broad capture was red only on the unrelated topology diagnostic for seed 3749; the admitted UI/control release scope passed when that separate assertion was explicitly excluded.
Physical-device feel remains an acceptance surface that automation cannot claim complete.
next: Collect the operator's physical-iPhone screenshots and control-feel result against production.
Complete the card only if the physical-device acceptance criteria pass; otherwise journal the exact residual and continue the same card.
blockers: Physical iPhone post-release verification remains pending.
